name.en: Downtown Core: Bathhouse Row & Central Avenue

summary.en: The historic heart of Hot Springs, this area is an open-air museum where the raw power of thermal springs was tamed into an elegant health resort, showcasing Beaux-Arts, Art Deco, and Spanish Revival styles.
Once a thriving commercial and social hub known as "Black Broadway," this district offers a quiet, contemplative walk through the city’s vital African American history.
A secret garden northwest of downtown, this neighborhood is built around a long, green park and lined with beautiful Queen Anne and Craftsman homes, perfect for a peaceful architectural stroll.

description.en: The only brewery in a U.S. National Park, using thermal spring water as its main ingredient in a restored 1916 bathhouse.
The most opulent historic bathhouse, now a free museum showcasing stunning stained-glass ceilings and vintage hydrotherapy equipment.
A true time capsule, this bathhouse has been in continuous operation since 1912, offering traditional thermal mineral baths.
An unmissable Art Deco skyscraper from 1929 whose detailed facade provides a beautiful contrast to the surrounding architecture.
A 216-foot observation tower offering a breathtaking 360-degree panorama of the city and the Ouachita Mountains.
A beautiful and historically significant landmark that has served as a cornerstone of the African American community for generations.
An impressive brick building that was a hub for Black-owned businesses and a monument to Black entrepreneurship.
A collection of 93 historic buildings where the real treasure is walking slowly to notice the variety of brickwork and architectural styles.
A linear park that serves as the neighborhood's spine, perfect for a stroll under mature trees to admire historic homes.
A living catalog of early 20th-century residential styles, including Queen Anne homes with wraparound porches and Craftsman houses with low-pitched roofs.

name.en: Arkansas Alligator Farm and Petting Zoo

description.en: A delightfully strange and historic old-school roadside attraction founded in 1902, featuring hundreds of alligators.
A surprising architectural anomaly featuring log cabins in the rustic Adirondack style, a world away from the nearby Victorian homes.

prompt.en: Take a photo of the most opulent and detailed piece of architecture you can find. Focus on the ambition of the design.
Find a building with Art Deco style. Capture its strong vertical lines and geometric details in a single shot.
Find a visual representation of water in the neighborhood—it could be a fountain, steam, or even a symbol carved in stone.
Take a selfie in the reflection of a historic storefront window, capturing both yourself and the history behind you.
Frame a shot that includes both a historic building and a modern element, like a new car or contemporary fashion.
Point your camera skyward and capture the ornate roofline or cornice of one of the grand old buildings on Central Avenue.
Let your senses guide you! Find a local snack or drink and take a picture of it with a historic backdrop.
Find a sign, plaque, or detail that tells you the age of a building and capture it.
Capture the interesting way sunlight and shadows play across the detailed facade of a building on Bathhouse Row.
Take a photo that captures the movement and energy of people exploring the historic downtown.
Find a spot where the green of the National Park meets the stone of the city and capture the contrast.
Find the most interesting and ornate doorway you can and take a photo. What stories could it tell?
Find a wall with interesting brickwork. Take a close-up shot that highlights its texture, color, and age.
Take a photo that you feel captures the spirit of the former "Black Broadway." What does community resilience look like?
Find a quiet spot in the district and take a photo that conveys a sense of peace and history.
Photograph a building that looks like it has stood the test of time. Focus on details that show its age and history.
Find a building that feels like the heart of the neighborhood, like a church or community center, and capture its presence.
Take a respectful selfie with one of the historic homes or buildings in the background, reflecting on the stories it holds.
Even in a quiet historic district, life goes on. Capture a modern detail against a historic backdrop.
Find an interesting window on one of the historic buildings. What might have been seen through it 100 years ago?
Look for a faded sign, worn steps, or peeling paint that hints at the vibrant life of the past. Capture this detail.
Create a single image or a collage of three different roof styles you can see in the district.
Take a photo down one of the streets, showing how the collection of historic buildings creates a unique landscape.
Find and photograph a cornerstone or foundation of a building. What year does it say? What does it represent?
Find the most charming and inviting porch you can see and take a photo. Focus on details like swings, columns, or plants.
Find a lush, green spot in the park or a beautiful garden and take a selfie enjoying the peaceful atmosphere.
Find a detail you think is classic Craftsman style (like a heavy column) and another that is classic Queen Anne (like a turret).
This neighborhood has some quirky spots. Find something unexpected—a strange statue, a weird mailbox, a surprising animal—and snap a pic.
Use the trees and paths of Whittington Park to frame a shot of one of the historic homes.
Many homes in this area have decorative windows. Find a piece of stained or leaded glass and capture how it catches the light.
Inspired by the Fordyce-Ricks House, find a texture in the neighborhood that feels rustic—it could be wood, stone, or something else.
Take a photo of a walking path in the park. Use it as a leading line to create a sense of a peaceful journey.
Find a house or a detail that seems different from all its neighbors. What makes it stand out?
Capture a photo that illustrates the cool shade provided by the mature trees in the park or along the streets.
Walk the residential streets and pick out the house you'd most want to live in. Take a photo of its most appealing feature.
The report mentions public thermal water fountains. See if you can spot one or any sign of the thermal water in this neighborhood.
